citation_spans: Item 8, Financial Statements, Income Statements — Provision for income taxes and Income before income taxes for fiscal year ended June 30, 2025

common_distractors_notes: 15% = rounded MD&A headline ('Revenue increased ... or 15%'); acceptable but we want one decimal precision. 13% = P&BP segment growth. 21% = IC segment growth. 23% = Microsoft Cloud growth. 17% = Q2 FY26 total revenue growth (wrong period). 26% = Microsoft Cloud Q2 FY26. 34% = Azure growth.

evidentiary_anchors.summary: Top-line growth: total revenue of $281.7B in FY2025, up 15% from $245.1B in FY2024, with growth across all three reporting segments. Operating income of $128.5B, up 17%.

mentions operating income in the $128-129B range OR op income growth near +17%
mentions growth being broad-based across all three reporting segments
evidentiary_anchors.not_engagement: generic 'revenue grew strongly' without figures or segment-wide framing

evidentiary_anchors.summary: AI-infrastructure capex is the dominant pressure on gross margin. Microsoft Cloud gross margin percentage decreased to 69% driven by the impact of scaling AI infrastructure, offset in part by Azure efficiency gains. Similar pressure called out in both Productivity and Business Processes and Intelligent Cloud segment gross margin commentary.

evidentiary_anchors.not_engagement: generic 'margins are under pressure' without naming AI infrastructure as driver

Quantifies demand visibility AND concentration profile simultaneously — engagement recognized from either interpretation (secured multi-year demand OR customer-concentration exposure).

evidentiary_anchors.summary: DEMAND — AI product adoption. 4.7M paid Copilot subscribers (+75% YoY); 15M paid Microsoft 365 Copilot seats; M365 Copilot seat adds +160% YoY; Copilot Pro Plus for individual devs +77% QoQ; M365 Copilot DAU 10x YoY; 24B Copilot interactions audited by Purview (+9x). Azure AI Foundry hosts broadest model catalog of any hyperscaler including GPT-5.0.2 AND Claude 4.5; 1,500+ customers actively use both Anthropic and OpenAI models on Foundry; Agent 365 extends Microsoft's identity/security/management stack to agents. Engagement recognized from either interpretation (Azure as durable substrate for agent economy whose consumption scales with every agent call OR Azure as commoditized inference provider reselling competitors' models with thin differentiation).

evidentiary_anchors.summary: SUBSTITUTION — per-unit risk disclosures. Item 1A explicit competitive risk: 'firms ... developing, making available, or using AI models that are open' as a competing business model; Item 1 Azure competition list includes 'cloud service providers and open source offerings' plus 'other emerging competitors and other open source offerings, many of which are also current or potential partners'; search/news advertising ex-TAC grew 10% CC 'slightly below expectations' in Q2 FY2026 with disclosed 'execution challenges.' Substitution intensity varies materially by unit (Bing/search vs Azure vs M365 vs LinkedIn).

evidentiary_anchors.not_engagement: generic 'AI faces competition' without per-unit differentiation or Item 1A citation

evidentiary_anchors.summary: SUPPLY CONSTRAINT — Azure. AI-driven capex ramp: $64.6B FY25 additions to property and equipment (vs $44.5B FY24 and $28.1B FY23); Q2 FY26 single-quarter capex $37.5B with approximately two-thirds on short-lived assets (GPUs/CPUs); $32.1B committed for datacenter construction at fiscal year-end. Management explicit: 'customer demand continues to exceed our supply' (Amy Hood). Item 7 MD&A names 'permitted and buildable land, predictable energy, networking supplies, and servers, including graphics processing units (GPUs) and other components' as supply dependencies. Engagement recognized from either interpretation (capacity-bound growth = pricing power + insurmountable scale moat OR capacity-bound growth = massive stranded-capex risk if demand shape shifts, with 6-year depreciation on short-lived GPUs).
evidentiary_anchors.citation_span: Item 8 Cash Flows Statements — Additions to property and equipment; Note 6 Property and Equipment; Item 7 MD&A — Economic Conditions and Other Planned Uses of Capital; Earnings call Q2 FY2026 — Amy Hood on capex allocation and supply constraint

mentions ~2/3 of Q2 FY26 capex on short-lived assets (GPUs/CPUs)
mentions $32.1B committed for datacenter construction at fiscal year end
quotes or paraphrases Amy Hood: 'customer demand continues to exceed our supply'
mentions supply dependencies (land, energy, networking, GPUs) from Item 7 MD&A
either framing: capacity-bound as moat / pricing power OR stranded-capex risk on short-lived GPU depreciation
evidentiary_anchors.not_engagement: generic 'capex is growing' without supply-constraint framing or specific figures

evidentiary_anchors.summary: UNIT ECONOMICS / margin trajectory. Microsoft Cloud gross margin 69% in FY2025 (decreased) with decline explicitly attributed to scaling AI infrastructure, offset in part by Azure efficiency gains; Q2 FY2026 company gross margin 68% down slightly YoY from same driver; Q3 FY2026 Microsoft Cloud gross margin expected 'roughly 65% down year over year driven by continued investments in AI.' Silicon-side response: Maya 200 accelerator claimed to deliver 30%+ TCO improvement over current fleet at FP4 precision. Engagement recognized from either interpretation (temporary margin compression with efficiency recovery as fleet matures OR structural margin re-rate as AI changes unit economics permanently).

evidentiary_anchors.summary: ROIC / strategic risk — management's own framing. Item 1A: 'We make significant investments in products and services that may not achieve expected returns... New products and services may not be profitable or may not achieve operating margins as high as we have experienced historically.' Companion: 'Our focus on cloud-based and AI services presents execution and competitive risks. We are incurring significant costs to build and maintain infrastructure to support cloud-based and AI services, reducing operating margins.' These are management's own articulation of the downside case — engagement recognized whether the thesis dismisses them (scale + distribution overcomes) or centers them (capital allocation risk dominates).
